---

title: "D02_00: Grid Initialization"
subtitle: "Create filtered schema for customer view storage"
parent: "D02_filtered_customer_views"
chapter: "CH12"
category: "derivation-task"
number: "D02_00"
date-created: "2025-12-24"
date-modified: "2025-12-24"
author: "Claude"
type: "derivation-task"
depends_on:
  - "D01_dna_analysis"
outputs_to:
  - "processed_data.filtered (schema)"
format:
  html:
    toc: true
    toc-depth: 3
    code-fold: false
---

# D02_00: Grid Initialization {#overview}

This task creates the filtered schema that will store all virtual views for customer filtering. It establishes the foundation for the multi-dimensional filtering system.

## Schema Creation (NSQL) {#schema-creation}

```nsql
# D02_00: Create Filtered Schema
CREATE_SCHEMA D02_00_filtered:
  SCHEMA_NAME: processed_data.filtered
  LOCATION: "data/local_data/processed_data.duckdb"

  PURPOSE: "Store virtual filtered views for customer analysis"

  PROPERTIES:
    storage_type: VIRTUAL  # Views, not materialized tables
    naming_convention: "df_sales_{source}_{time}_{product}_{state}"
    access_pattern: READ_ONLY

  ON_EXISTS: SKIP  # Don't recreate if exists
```

## Prerequisites

| Requirement | Description |
|-------------|-------------|
| D01 Complete | Customer DNA profiles must exist |
| Database Access | Connection to processed_data.duckdb |
| Write Permission | Ability to create schema |

## Filter Dimension Schema (NSQL) {#filter-schema}

```nsql
# D02 Filter Dimension Schema Definition
DEFINE FILTER_DIMENSIONS:
  time_condition:
    TYPE: ENUM
    VALUES: ['now', 'm1year', 'm1quarter', 'm1month', 'm2year', 'ytd']
    DEFAULT: 'now'
    DESCRIPTION: "Time range filter for historical data"

  product_line_id_filter:
    TYPE: INTEGER
    VALUES: [0, 1, 2, 3, ...]  # 0 = all products
    DEFAULT: 0
    DESCRIPTION: "Product line category filter"

  state_filter:
    TYPE: VARCHAR
    VALUES: ['ALL', 'CA', 'NY', 'TX', ...]  # ALL = all regions
    DEFAULT: 'ALL'
    DESCRIPTION: "Geographic region filter"

  source_filter:
    TYPE: VARCHAR
    VALUES: ['amazon', 'ebay', 'retail', 'cbz']
    DESCRIPTION: "Platform/channel data source"

# Special value handling
DEFINE SPECIAL_VALUES:
  time_condition.now: "No time restriction, all historical data"
  product_line_id_filter.0: "All products across all lines"
  state_filter.ALL: "All geographic regions combined"
```

## Schema Properties {#properties}

| Property | Value | Description |
|----------|-------|-------------|
| Storage Type | VIRTUAL | Views, not materialized tables |
| Naming Convention | `df_sales_{source}_{time}_{product}_{state}` | Consistent naming |
| Access Pattern | READ_ONLY | Views are read-only |
| On Exists | SKIP | Don't recreate if exists |

## Output Schema Structure

```nsql
# Schema: processed_data.filtered
SCHEMA processed_data.filtered:
  CONTAINS:
    # Sales views (one per condition combination)
    df_sales_{condition_key}:
      COLUMNS: [all columns from source + filter columns]
      TYPE: VIRTUAL VIEW

    # Customer-centric views (one per condition combination)
    df_sales_by_customer_{condition_key}:
      COLUMNS: [customer_id, aggregated metrics, filter columns]
      TYPE: VIRTUAL VIEW
```

## Validation (NSQL) {#validation}

```nsql
# D02_00: Validate Schema Creation
VALIDATE D02_00:
  CHECK schema_exists:
    QUERY: "SELECT schema_name FROM information_schema.schemata WHERE schema_name = 'filtered'"
    ASSERT: COUNT(*) = 1
    MESSAGE: "Filtered schema must exist"

  ON_SUCCESS: LOG "D02_00 completed: filtered schema created"
  ON_FAILURE: ABORT "D02_00 failed: could not create schema"
```

## Related Documentation

- [D02_01_condition_grid.qmd](D02_01_condition_grid.qmd) - Grid generation
- [index.qmd](index.qmd) - D02 overview
